metadata.source: Public historical records, government white papers, statistical yearbooks, and academic archives
metadata.description: Comprehensive dataset of Chinese actors (organizations and individuals) with a focus on policy, economic, and research milestones from 1900 to 2025.
metadata.introduction: A granular representation of the evolution of key actors within the social region of China. This dataset traces the formation, transformation, and significant milestones of sovereign, corporate, and scientific entities, highlighting the transition from early industrialization to a modern innovation-driven economy.

actors.name: National Development and Reform Commission (NDRC)
actors.description: The principal economic planning agency responsible for drafting national economic and social development plans.

events.description: Established as the State Planning Commission.
Reorganized into the State Development Planning Commission.
Merged with other bodies to form the NDRC.
Announced 4 trillion RMB stimulus package in response to global financial crisis.
Released the 12th Five-Year Plan focusing on strategic emerging industries.
Issued guidelines for the 'Internet Plus' action plan.
Released the 13th Five-Year Plan for Economic and Social Development.
Released the 14th Five-Year Plan outlining vision for 2035.
Released plan for 'New Infrastructure' construction focusing on digital economy.

actors.name: Chinese Academy of Sciences (CAS)
actors.description: The supreme national body for scientific research, overseeing hundreds of research institutes.

events.description: Founded in Beijing following the establishment of the PRC.
Establishment of the Academic Divisions (CAS Members).
Played key role in founding the University of Science and Technology of China (USTC).
Hosted the National Science Conference, marking the 'Spring of Science'.
Began reforms to integrate science with economy.
Launch of the 'Knowledge Innovation Program'.
Contributed to the success of Shenzhou 5 manned space mission.
Launch of the Strategic Priority Research Program.
CAS scientist Tu Youyou awarded Nobel Prize in Physiology or Medicine.
Launched 'Micius' (QUESS), the world's first quantum science satellite.
Completion of the China Sky Eye (FAST) telescope.
Provided key payloads for the Shenzhou-12 mission to the Tiangong space station.

actors.name: Peking University
actors.description: A prestigious comprehensive university, historically central to modern Chinese thought and science.

events.description: Founded as Imperial University of Peking.
Renamed Peking University.
Students initiated the May Fourth Movement, a cultural and political modernization movement.
Relocated to Changsha, forming National Changsha Temporary University.
Formed National Southwest Associated University with Tsinghua and Nankai.
Became a comprehensive university focusing on basic sciences after nationwide restructuring.
Celebrated centenary; '985 Project' initiated to build world-class universities.
Merged with Beijing Medical University to form Peking University Health Science Center.
Launch of the 'Yenching Scholars' program for global humanities studies.
Selected for Ministry of Education's 'Double First-Class' university plan.

actors.name: Bank of China (BOC)
actors.description: One of the 'Big Four' state-owned commercial banks, historically the bank for foreign exchange.

events.description: Established by the Republican government, succeeding the Ta-Ching Government Bank.
Reorganized as an international exchange bank.
Taken over by the People's Republic government.
Designated as the state specialized foreign exchange bank.
Transformed into a wholly state-owned commercial bank.
Listed on the Hong Kong Stock Exchange (SEHK) and Shanghai Stock Exchange (SSE).
Served as Official Banking Partner of the Beijing 2008 Olympic Games.
Became the first Chinese bank to be recognized as a Global Systemically Important Bank (G-SIB) in the FSB list.
Issued the first digital RMB wallet to a consumer in Beijing.

actors.name: Chinese Academy of Engineering (CAE)
actors.description: The highest honorary and advisory institution in China's engineering sector.

events.description: Established in Beijing.
First cohort of academicians elected.
Established the Center for International Engineering Education.
Celebrated 20th anniversary; issued report on manufacturing strategy.
Jointly released strategy on 'Made in China 2025' technical roadmaps.
Advisory role in rapid construction of Huoshenshan Hospital.

actors.name: State Grid Corporation of China
actors.description: The largest utility company in the world, managing the vast majority of China's electricity grid.

events.description: Predecessor Ministry of Electric Power established.
Ministry dissolved; formation of State Power Corporation.
State Grid Corporation of China officially established in power sector reform.
Commissioned the Jindongnan-Nanyang-Jingmen UHV AC transmission line.
Xiangjiaba-Shanghai UHV DC transmission line put into operation.
Achieved full electrification of the 'last households' in Tibet.
Acquired controlling stake in CPFL Energia in Brazil.
Launched the 'Global Energy Interconnection' initiative.
Commissioned the Qinghai-Henan UHV DC project (world's first UHV for renewables).

actors.name: Huawei Technologies
actors.description: A leading global provider of information and communications technology (ICT) infrastructure and smart devices.

events.description: Founded in Shenzhen by Ren Zhengfei as a private firm.
First major contract with China Telecom.
First overseas contract in Netherlands for UMTS network.
Became world's second-largest telecom equipment maker.
Surpassed Ericsson to become world's largest telecom equipment maker.
Added to US Entity List, restricting access to US technology.
Launched HarmonyOS operating system.
Sold Honor brand to a consortium to ensure survival of the brand.
Announced entry into intelligent automotive components market.
Launched Mate 60 series with advanced domestic chip capabilities.

actors.name: Ministry of Science and Technology (MOST)
actors.description: The executive agency responsible for formulating science and technology policy and implementing national S&T programs.

events.description: State Technology Commission established.
Merged to form State Science and Technology Commission (SSTC).
SSTC re-established after Cultural Revolution.
Initiated the National High-tech R&D Program (863 Program).
Launched the Torch Program for high-tech industrialization.
Reorganized into Ministry of Science and Technology (MOST).
Initiated the Medium and Long-Term Plan for S&T Development (2006-2020).
Released outline of national innovation-driven development strategy.
Released 14th Five-Year Plan for S&T development.

actors.name: China National Space Administration (CNSA)
actors.description: The government agency responsible for the national space program and international space cooperation.

events.description: Established as a government agency.
Successful launch of Shenzhou 1 (unmanned test flight).
First manned spaceflight (Shenzhou 5) with astronaut Yang Liwei.
Launched Chang'e 1, China's first lunar probe.
First spacewalk by Chinese astronaut (Zhai Zhigang on Shenzhou 7).
Launch of Tiangong-1 space laboratory.
Chang'e 3 successfully lands rover Yutu on the Moon.
Chang'e 4 makes first-ever soft landing on far side of the Moon.
Final satellite of BeiDou Navigation Satellite System launched.
Launched Tianwen-1 Mars mission.
Chang'e 5 returns lunar samples to Earth.
Tianwen-1 lander and Zhurong rover successfully land on Mars.
Completion of the Tiangong Space Station (T-shape configuration).

actors.name: Tsinghua University
actors.description: A major public research university, renowned for engineering and science, often called 'China's MIT'.

events.description: Founded as Tsing Hua Imperial College.
Renamed National Tsing Hua University.
Became a polytechnic institute focused on engineering after restructuring.
Operations suspended during the Cultural Revolution.
Resumed enrollment and restored science and engineering departments.
Established the School of Economics and Management.
Merged with the Central Academy of Arts and Design.
Celebrated 100th anniversary.
Launch of the Tsinghua University Global Innovation Exchange (GIX) in Seattle.
Established the Institute for AI International Governance.

actors.name: China Petroleum & Chemical Corporation (Sinopec)
actors.description: One of the largest oil and gas companies in the world.

events.description: China Petrochemical Corporation (Sinopec Group) established.
China Petroleum & Chemical Corporation incorporated for listing.
Listed on Hong Kong, New York, and London stock exchanges.
Listed on Shanghai Stock Exchange.
Commissioned the Sichuan-East China Gas Pipeline.
Breakthrough in coal-to-olefins technology commercialization.
Commenced operation of the world's largest ethylene plant in Zhoushan.
Rapidly shifted production to medical melt-blown fabric for masks during pandemic.

actors.name: Alibaba Group
actors.description: Multinational technology company specializing in e-commerce, retail, internet, and technology.

events.description: Founded in Hangzhou by Jack Ma and 17 others.
Launched Taobao Marketplace.
Launched Alipay payment platform.
Listed on Hong Kong Stock Exchange (B2B unit).
Inaugural 'Double 11' (Singles' Day) shopping festival.
Record-breaking IPO on New York Stock Exchange.
Opened first 'Hema' (Freshippo) retail store.
Secondary listing on Hong Kong Stock Exchange.
Acquired Sun Art Retail Group to expand grocery footprint.
Announced major restructuring into six independent business units.

actors.name: China Development Bank (CDB)
actors.description: A development finance institution funding key national projects like infrastructure and export industries.

events.description: Established as one of three policy banks.
Shifted focus to infrastructure and bottleneck industries.
Issued first Panda Bond (RMB-denominated bond by foreign entity).
Converted into a joint-stock limited liability company.
Major financing support for high-speed railway network expansion.
Key role in financing 'Belt and Road' Initiative projects.
Financed major photovoltaic and wind power base projects.

actors.name: Institute of Computing Technology, CAS
actors.description: The first computer research institute in China, birthplace of domestic CPU and supercomputers.

events.description: Established as China's first computer technology research institute.
Developed China's first digital electronic computer (103).
Developed the Galaxy-I (Yinhe-I) supercomputer.
Developed the 'Dragon' (Loongson) general-purpose CPU.
Formed the Loongson Technologies company for commercialization.
Loongson architecture recognized as mature CPU architecture.
Launched Loongson 3A5000 processor with self-designed architecture.

actors.name: Beijing Genomics Institute (BGI)
actors.description: One of the world's premier genomics research centers.

events.description: Founded in Beijing to participate in the Human Genome Project.
Completed 1% of the Human Genome Project.
Decoded the SARS virus genome rapidly.
Headquarters moved to Shenzhen.
Purchased 128 HiSeq 2000 sequencers, becoming world's largest sequencing center.
Acquired Complete Genomics for DNA sequencing technology.
BGI Genomics listed on Shenzhen Stock Exchange.
Rapid sequencing and global sharing of COVID-19 genome.

actors.name: University of Science and Technology of China (USTC)
actors.description: A top research university established to address urgent science and technology needs.

events.description: Founded in Beijing under CAS.
Relocated to Hefei, Anhui Province.
Established the first 'Special Class for the Gifted Young' (SCGY).
Selected for '985 Project'.
Selected for 'Double First-Class' initiative.
Achievements in quantum communication research (Pan Jianwei team).

actors.name: China Railway Rolling Stock Corporation (CRRC)
actors.description: The world's largest supplier of rail transit equipment.

events.description: Predecessor Ministry of Railways factories established.
Restructured into China National Railway Locomotive & Rolling Stock Industry Corp.
Split into CSR (China South) and CNR (China North).
Delivery of CRH high-speed trains for 'Harmony' (Hexie) lines.
Merger of CNR and CSR to form CRRC.
Launch of 'Fuxing' (Rejuvenation) EMU trains with Chinese IP.
Launch of 600 km/h maglev train prototype.
600 km/h high-speed maglev transportation system debuted.

actors.name: Tencent Holdings
actors.description: A leading global provider of internet value-added services.

events.description: Founded in Shenzhen.
Listed on the Main Board of Hong Kong Stock Exchange.
Launched Weixin (WeChat) messaging app.
Launched WeChat Pay, revolutionizing mobile payments.
Invested heavily in AI and medical imaging labs.
Announced strategic upgrade to 'Industrial Internet'.
Compliance with new data privacy regulations.

actors.name: National Natural Science Foundation of China (NSFC)
actors.description: An organization supporting basic research and fostering scientific talent.

events.description: Established as the National Natural Science Foundation of China.
Implemented the 'Young Teacher Fund'.
Launched the 'National Outstanding Youth Science Fund'.
Budget exceeded 2 billion RMB.
International cooperation budget increased significantly.
Budget exceeded 30 billion RMB.
Reform to support 'from 0 to 1' original innovation.

actors.name: BYD Company Limited
actors.description: A major Chinese manufacturing company known for electric vehicles and batteries.

events.description: Founded in Shenzhen as a battery manufacturer.
Listed on Hong Kong Stock Exchange.
Acquired Xi'an Qinchuan Automobile to enter auto industry.
Launched the F3DM, the world's first mass-produced plug-in hybrid.
Opened US headquarters in Los Angeles.
Started sales of 'Dynasty' series electric vehicles.
Launched 'Blade Battery' technology.
Surpassed Tesla to become world's top EV seller by volume (half-year).
Announced sales in Japan and European markets.

actors.name: Zhejiang University
actors.description: A prestigious comprehensive university with strong engineering and science programs.

events.description: Founded as Qiushi Academy.
Renamed National Zhejiang University.
Relocated westward during war.
Restructured into engineering-focused institution.
Merged with Hangzhou University, Zhejiang Agricultural University, and Zhejiang Medical University.
Established International Campus in Haining.
Establishment of the Innovation Center for Yangtze River Delta.

actors.name: China Aerospace Science and Technology Corporation (CASC)
actors.description: The main contractor for the Chinese space program.

events.description: Fifth Academy of the Ministry of National Defense established (predecessor).
Successfully launched Dong Fang Hong I, China's first satellite.
Ministry of Aerospace Industry established.
Split into CASC and CASIC.
First Beidou navigation satellite launched.
Successfully tested new generation launch vehicle engine.
Developer of the Long March 5 rocket for Mars mission.

actors.name: Shanghai Institute of Materia Medica (SIMM)
actors.description: A leading institute in drug discovery and development.

events.description: Founded as National Peiping Academy of Sciences.
Reorganized under CAS as Shanghai Institute of Materia Medica.
First total synthesis of bovine insulin.
Discovered Artemether (anti-malarial drug).
Launched National Key Laboratory of Drug Research.
Screening of potential COVID-19 drug candidates.

actors.name: Industrial and Commercial Bank of China (ICBC)
actors.description: The largest bank in the world by total assets.

events.description: Established as a state-owned specialized bank.
Converted to a joint-stock limited company.
Simultaneous IPO in Hong Kong and Shanghai, world's largest at the time.
Establishment of ICBC Middle East.
Acquired Standard Bank Argentina.
Piloted digital RMB consumption scenarios.

actors.name: Sany Heavy Industry
actors.description: A leading global heavy equipment manufacturer.

events.description: Founded in Lianyuan, Hunan.
Listed on Shanghai Stock Exchange.
Acquired Putzmeister, the German concrete pump maker.
Established US manufacturing plant in Georgia.
First Chinese machinery company to exceed 100 billion RMB revenue.
Unveiled the world's largest tonnage excavator.

actors.name: Institute of Semiconductors, CAS
actors.description: A key institute for semiconductor technology development.

events.description: Established in Beijing.
Developed China's first planar transistor.
Developed first semiconductor laser.
Establishment of National Engineering Research Center for Optoelectronics.
Major breakthroughs in silicon-based optoelectronics.
Development of high-performance integrated photonic chips.

actors.name: Ministry of Industry and Information Technology (MIIT)
actors.description: Agency responsible for regulating and promoting the industrial and information technology sectors.

events.description: Formed by merging the Commission of Science, Technology and Industry for National Defense and Ministry of Information Industry.
Promulgated guidelines for Strategic Emerging Industries.
Issued 4G (TD-LTE) licenses to carriers.
Released 'Made in China 2025' strategic plan.
Issued 5G commercial licenses to carriers.
Implemented Data Security Law of the PRC.
Implemented Personal Information Protection Law.

actors.name: Harbin Institute of Technology (HIT)
actors.description: A key university for engineering and aerospace research.

events.description: Founded as Harbin Sino-Russian School for Industry.
Designated as one of the first six key national universities.
Relocation of some departments to Chongqing (Second HIT).
Selected for '211 Project'.
Selected for '985 Project'.
Key contributor to the final satellite of BeiDou-3 system.

actors.name: DJI (Da-Jiang Innovations)
actors.description: A global leader in civilian drones and aerial imaging technology.

events.description: Founded by Frank Wang in a dorm room.
Released the first Phantom drone prototype.
Phantom 1 launched, kickstarting the consumer drone market.
Released Phantom 3 with integrated down-vision sensors.
Launched Mavic 2 series.
Developed drone solutions for epidemic prevention.
Launched Mini 3 Pro drone.

actors.name: Wuhan Institute of Virology (WIV)
actors.description: A research institute under CAS focused on virology and bio-safety.

events.description: Established as Wuhan Microbiology Laboratory.
Renamed Wuhan Institute of Virology.
Active research on SARS coronavirus.
Biosafety Level 4 (BSL-4) lab inaugurated.
Identified and shared genome sequence of SARS-CoV-2.

actors.name: China State Construction Engineering Corporation (CSCEC)
actors.description: The largest construction company in the world by revenue.

events.description: Predecessor organization established.
Incorporated as China State Construction Engineering Corporation.
Construction arm listed on Shanghai Stock Exchange.
Completed the 'Water Cube' (National Aquatics Center) for Beijing Olympics.
Major contracts under 'Belt and Road' Initiative.
Completed construction of Beijing Daxing International Airport.
Completed Huoshenshan Hospital in 10 days.

actors.name: Fudan University
actors.description: A prestigious comprehensive research university.

events.description: Founded as Fudan Public School.
Became a private university.
Became a national university.
Restructured into a comprehensive university of arts and sciences.
Merged with Shanghai Medical University.
Launch of the International Center for Quantum Materials.

actors.name: Baidu, Inc.
actors.description: A leading AI company and search engine provider.

events.description: Founded in Beijing by Robin Li.
Listed on NASDAQ.
Launched Baidu Maps.
Restructured to focus on AI; established Baidu AI Cloud.
Launched Apollo, an open-source autonomous driving platform.
Secondary listing on Hong Kong Stock Exchange.
Launched 'Ernie Bot' (Wenxin Yiyan), a large language model.

actors.name: Institute of Botany, CAS
actors.description: A comprehensive research institute for basic and applied plant sciences.

events.description: Established as the Institute of Botany, Peiping Academy of Sciences.
Reorganized under CAS.
Establishment of National Key Laboratory of Systematic and Evolutionary Botany.
Relocation to new campus at CAS Innovation Park.
Major contributions to plant genome sequencing projects.

actors.name: Contemporary Amperex Technology Co. Limited (CATL)
actors.description: A global leader in lithium-ion battery development and manufacturing.

events.description: Founded in Ningde, Fujian.
Supplied batteries for BMW electrification.
Listed on Shenzhen Stock Exchange.
Announced investment in Jiangxi lithium mining site.
Announced battery plant in Hungary (Europe's largest).
Announced partnership with Ford for US battery plant.

actors.name: Ministry of Ecology and Environment
actors.description: Cabinet-level ministry responsible for environmental protection.

events.description: Predecessor National Environmental Protection Agency established.
Upgraded to State Environmental Protection Administration (SEPA).
Became Ministry of Environmental Protection.
Upgraded to Ministry of Ecology and Environment (MEE).
Announced 'Dual Carbon' goals (Peak 2030, Neutrality 2060).
Launch of national carbon emissions trading market (ETS).

actors.name: Shanghai Stock Exchange (SSE)
actors.description: One of the three stock exchanges in mainland China.

events.description: Established.
Commenced official operations.
Launched ChiNext board for growth enterprises.
Launched Shanghai-Hong Kong Stock Connect.
Launched STAR Market (Science and Technology Innovation Board).
Market capitalization exceeded 50 trillion RMB.

actors.name: China Earthquake Administration (CEA)
actors.description: Government agency for earthquake monitoring and research.

events.description: Established as the National Earthquake Bureau.
Renamed China Seismological Bureau.
Response to Wenchuan Earthquake.
Renamed China Earthquake Administration.
Implementation of National Earthquake Intensity Rapid Reporting and Early Warning Project.

actors.name: Geely Automobile Holdings
actors.description: A leading Chinese automobile manufacturer.

events.description: Founded by Li Shufu as a refrigerator parts manufacturer.
Entered the automotive industry.
Listed on Hong Kong Stock Exchange.
Signed agreement to acquire Volvo Car Corporation from Ford.
Acquired 9.69% stake in Daimler AG.
Launch of Zeekr, a premium electric vehicle brand.

actors.name: Shanghai Jiao Tong University
actors.description: A major public research university with strong engineering and medical schools.

events.description: Founded as Nanyang Public School.
Renamed Jiaotong University.
Most departments relocated to Xi'an to form Xi'an Jiaotong University.
Shanghai part independently named Shanghai Jiao Tong University.
Merged with Shanghai Second Medical University.
Opened the student innovation center 'Zero One'.

actors.name: Xiaomi Corporation
actors.description: A major electronics company known for smartphones and IoT ecosystem.

events.description: Founded in Beijing by Lei Jun.
Launched first Xiaomi phone.
Launched Mi TV, entering smart home market.
Listed on Main Board of Hong Kong Stock Exchange.
Announced entry into electric vehicle market.
Launch of Xiaomi SU7 electric vehicle (Planned date per user prompt scope extending to 2025).

actors.name: China Baowu Steel Group
actors.description: The world's largest steel producer.

events.description: Baosteel Group founded.
Baoshan Iron & Steel listed on Shanghai Stock Exchange.
Merger of Baosteel and Wuhan Iron & Steel to form China Baowu.
Surpassed ArcelorMittal as world's largest steel producer.
Pledge to achieve carbon peaking by 2023 and carbon neutrality by 2050.

actors.name: Dalian Institute of Chemical Physics (DICP)
actors.description: A multidisciplinary institute focusing on energy and chemical engineering.

events.description: Established as Dalian Institute of Scientific Research.
Merged with Institute of Petroleum Chemistry.
Key role in developing aerospace propellants.
Establishment of State Key Laboratory of Catalysis.
Achievement in liquid hydrogen fuel cells for vehicles.

actors.name: People's Bank of China (PBOC)
actors.description: The central bank of the People's Republic of China.

events.description: Established in Shijiazhuang.
Designated as central bank, handing commercial duties to ICBC.
Implemented exchange rate reform.
RMB added to IMF SDR basket.
Launched digital currency trials in Shenzhen, Suzhou, etc.
Replaced the head of the bank in a routine leadership transition.

actors.name: Nanjing University
actors.description: A prestigious university with strong physics and humanities programs.

events.description: Founded as Sanjiang Normal School.
Became Nanjing University after restructuring.
Published the first paper on the Hemudu culture discovery.
Establishment of Xianlin Campus.
Major contributions to condensed matter physics research.

actors.name: SenseTime
actors.description: A leading AI software company focused on computer vision and deep learning.

events.description: Founded by Tang Xiao'ou and colleagues.
Raised $410 million, valuing it at over $1.5 billion.
Announced plans for smart city projects.
Selected for National Open Innovation Platform for Intelligent Vision.
Listed on Hong Kong Stock Exchange.
Launched 'SenseNova' large model series.

actors.name: China National Nuclear Corporation (CNNC)
actors.description: A major state-owned enterprise overseeing China's nuclear industry.

events.description: Third Ministry of Machine Building established (predecessor).
First atomic reactor built.
China National Nuclear Corporation formed.
Qinshan Nuclear Power Plant connected to grid (first domestically designed).
Fuqing Unit 5, first Hualong One reactor, construction started.
Hualong One reactor entered commercial operation.

actors.name: Institute of Atmospheric Physics, CAS
actors.description: A leading institute for research on atmospheric science and climate change.

events.description: Predecessor Institute of Meteorology established.
Institute of Atmospheric Physics established.
Establishment of the Atmospheric Sounding Laboratory.
Launch of the 'Earth System Model' development.
Contributions to the Sixth Assessment Report of the IPCC.

actors.name: National Medical Products Administration (NMPA)
actors.description: Regulatory agency for drugs and medical devices.

events.description: Established as State Drug Administration.
Reorganized into State Food and Drug Administration (SFDA).
Became China Food and Drug Administration (CFDA).
Renamed National Medical Products Administration (NMPA).
Conditional approval of first domestic COVID-19 vaccine.

actors.name: University of Hong Kong (HKU)
actors.description: The territory's oldest institute of higher learning.

events.description: Incorporated by the Governor of Hong Kong.
First congregation for graduation.
Post-war reorganization.
Establishment of the Faculty of Dentistry (Hong Kong's only one).
Identified the SARS coronavirus.
Adaptation to National Security Law in curriculum and governance.

actors.name: China Mobile
actors.description: The world's largest mobile network operator by subscribers.

events.description: Incorporated in Hong Kong.
Listed on New York and Hong Kong stock exchanges.
Established China Mobile Communications Corporation (parent).
Received 4G (TD-LTE) license.
Received 5G commercial license.
US delisting under executive order; focused on mainland listings.

actors.name: Fosun International
actors.description: A global conglomerate focused on health, happiness, and wealth.

events.description: Founded by Guo Guangchang and others.
Listed on Hong Kong Stock Exchange.
Acquired Club Med.
Invested in Thomas Cook Group.
Partnership with BioNTech for COVID-19 vaccine (Comirnaty) in Greater China.
Completed acquisition of Yingchuang Dinostar (bio-tech).

actors.name: Institute of Psychology, CAS
actors.description: The only national-level research institute for psychology.

events.description: Established as Institute of Psychology, Academia Sinica.
Re-established under CAS after a suspension.
Activities suspended during Cultural Revolution.
Institute formally re-established.
Launch of National Key Laboratory of Mental Health.

actors.name: China Telecom Corporation
actors.description: A major state-owned telecommunications company.

events.description: Established as a separate entity from the Ministry of Posts and Telecommunications.
Restructured and listed in Hong Kong and New York.
Acquired China Unicom's CDMA network.
Returned to A-share listing on Shanghai Stock Exchange.
Major deployment of 'Gigabit Broadband' networks.

actors.name: Beijing Institute of Technology (BIT)
actors.description: A key university for engineering and defense technology.

events.description: Founded as the Academy of Natural Sciences in Yan'an.
Renamed School of Engineering.
Renamed Beijing Institute of Technology.
Selected for '211 Project'.
Selected for '985 Project'.
Major contributions to electric vehicle battery technology.

actors.name: SF Express
actors.description: A leading logistics service provider in China.

events.description: Founded in Shunde, Guangdong.
Began construction of its own cargo airline fleet.
Listed on Shenzhen Stock Exchange via backdoor listing.
Launched 'SF U-Pick' service for agriculture.
Acquired Kerry Logistics (majority stake) for global expansion.

actors.name: Institute of Geology and Geophysics, CAS
actors.description: A comprehensive institute for solid earth sciences.

events.description: Formed by merger of Institute of Geology and Institute of Geophysics.
Discovery of evidence for Mars water activity via meteorites.
Establishment of State Key Laboratory of Lithospheric Evolution.
Deep-Earth exploration drilling project achievements.

actors.name: China Securities Regulatory Commission (CSRC)
actors.description: The principal regulator of securities and futures markets.

events.description: Established by the State Council.
Securities Law enacted, formalizing CSRC powers.
Allowed domestic investors to trade B-shares.
Oversaw launch of Shanghai-Hong Kong Stock Connect.
Oversaw launch of STAR Market.
Implementation of comprehensive registration-based IPO system.

actors.name: Lenovo Group
actors.description: A global leader in personal computers and IT solutions.

events.description: Founded as 'New Technology Developer Inc.' by Liu Chuanzhi.
Launched first self-branded PC.
Listed on Hong Kong Stock Exchange.
Acquired IBM's Personal Computer Division.
Acquired IBM's x86 server business and Motorola Mobility.
Ranked #171 on Fortune Global 500.

actors.name: Wanhua Chemical Group
actors.description: A global leader in polyurethanes and chemical new materials.

events.description: Predecessor Yantai Synthetic Leather Factory established.
Incorporated as Yantai Wanhua Polyurethanes Co., Ltd.
Listed on Shanghai Stock Exchange.
Acquired BorsodChem (Hungary), expanding European footprint.
Became world's largest MDI producer.

actors.name: Tongji University
actors.description: Renowned for its engineering, architecture, and urban planning programs.

events.description: Founded as German Medical School.
Became National Tongji University.
Became a university specializing in civil engineering and architecture.
Merged with Shanghai Railway University.
Selected for 'Double First-Class' initiative.

actors.name: Institute of Hydrobiology, CAS
actors.description: Leading institute for aquatic life sciences and water environment protection.

events.description: Established as Natural History Museum of the Academia Sinica.
Relocated to Wuhan and renamed Institute of Hydrobiology.
Research on conservation of the Baiji (Yangtze River Dolphin).
First successful breeding of Chinese sturgeon in captivity.
Advisory role in the 10-year fishing ban on the Yangtze River.

actors.name: Ministry of Transport
actors.description: Responsible for road, water, and air transport administration.

events.description: Ministry of Communications established.
Restructured into Ministry of Transport.
Railway administration functions transferred from defunct Ministry of Railways.
Issued guidelines for new infrastructure construction (transport logistics).
Released planning outline for National Comprehensive立体 Transportation Network (2021-2035).

actors.name: China Minmetals Corporation
actors.description: A major state-owned metals and mining group.

events.description: Established as China National Metals & Minerals Import & Export Corp.
Restructured into China National Metals and Minerals Import and Export Corporation.
Acquired majority stake in Australia's OZ Minerals.
Strategic restructuring with China Metallurgical Group Corporation (MCC).
Operated one of the world's largest lithium processing facilities.

actors.name: JD.com (Jingdong)
actors.description: A major e-commerce company with a strong direct sales and logistics network.

events.description: Founded as a magneto-optical store in Beijing by Liu Qiangdong.
Pivoted to online retail.
Listed on NASDAQ.
Achieved first drone delivery to a rural village.
Secondary listing in Hong Kong.
Expansion of 'JD Logistics' to external clients.

actors.name: Institute of Neuroscience, CAS
actors.description: A premier institute for brain research.

events.description: Established in Shanghai.
Establishment of State Key Laboratory of Neuroscience.
Generated the world's first monkeys cloned from somatic cells (Zhong Zhong and Hua Hua).
